enonce.texte: Match the following volunteering and community service terms with their correct definitions. This exercise will help you master the key vocabulary needed to discuss volunteering.

questions.texte: Match each term (a-e) with its correct definition (i-v).

sousQuestions.texte: Volunteer
sousQuestions.reponse: A person who freely offers to take part in an enterprise or undertake a task without being paid.

sousQuestions.texte: Community service
sousQuestions.reponse: Voluntary work intended to help people in a particular area.

sousQuestions.texte: Skills
sousQuestions.reponse: The ability to do something well, usually gained through training or experience.

sousQuestions.texte: Experience
sousQuestions.reponse: Practical contact with and observation of facts or events.

sousQuestions.texte: Satisfaction
sousQuestions.reponse: Fulfillment of one's wishes, expectations, or needs, or the pleasure derived from this.

questions.texte: Fill in the blank with the correct word from the list: [volunteer, community service, skills, experience, satisfaction].
questions.reponse: Many young people choose to volunteer to gain valuable experience for their future careers.

sousQuestions.texte: Many young people choose to _______ to gain valuable _______ for their future careers.
sousQuestions.reponse: volunteer, experience

sousQuestions.texte: Participating in _______ helps develop important _______, such as leadership and communication.
sousQuestions.reponse: community service, skills

sousQuestions.texte: The greatest reward of volunteering is the personal _______ you feel from helping others.
sousQuestions.reponse: satisfaction

enonce.texte: Complete the following sentences using the infinitive of purpose (to + verb) to explain why people volunteer or take certain actions.

questions.texte: Complete each sentence with the infinitive of purpose.

sousQuestions.texte: He volunteered at the hospital _______ (help) sick children.
sousQuestions.reponse: to help

sousQuestions.texte: She joined the community clean-up project _______ (improve) her neighborhood.
sousQuestions.reponse: to improve

sousQuestions.texte: Many students participate in volunteer programs _______ (gain) practical experience.
sousQuestions.reponse: to gain

sousQuestions.texte: He started a youth organization _______ (address) the issue of drug abuse.
sousQuestions.reponse: to address

sousQuestions.texte: They organized a fundraising event _______ (raise) money for the school library.
sousQuestions.reponse: to raise

questions.texte: Rewrite the following sentences using the infinitive of purpose.

sousQuestions.texte: She volunteered at the clinic because she wanted to help patients.
sousQuestions.reponse: She volunteered at the clinic to help patients.

sousQuestions.texte: They started a recycling program because they wanted to reduce waste.
sousQuestions.reponse: They started a recycling program to reduce waste.

enonce.texte:
Read the following text about the benefits of volunteering and answer the questions below.

The Power of Volunteering

Volunteering is a powerful way to make a difference in your community while also developing yourself. Many people choose to volunteer because they want to give back to society. Through volunteering, individuals can develop important skills such as leadership, communication, and teamwork. These skills are valuable not only for personal growth but also for professional development.

Additionally, volunteering provides a sense of purpose and satisfaction. Helping others can boost self-esteem and reduce stress. For young people, volunteering can be a stepping stone to future career opportunities. It allows them to gain practical experience and build a network of contacts.

Community service also strengthens communities. When people work together to solve problems, they create stronger, more resilient neighborhoods. Volunteering is a win-win situation: volunteers gain valuable experience, and communities benefit from their contributions.

questions.texte: Answer the following questions based on the text.

sousQuestions.texte: Why do many people choose to volunteer according to the text?
sousQuestions.reponse: Because they want to give back to society.

sousQuestions.texte: What skills can volunteers develop through community service?
sousQuestions.reponse: Leadership, communication, and teamwork.

sousQuestions.texte: How does volunteering benefit young people specifically?
sousQuestions.reponse: It allows them to gain practical experience and build a network of contacts.

sousQuestions.texte: Explain why volunteering is described as a "win-win situation."
sousQuestions.reponse: Volunteering is a win-win situation because volunteers gain valuable experience and satisfaction, while communities benefit from their contributions.

intitule: Using the Infinitive of Purpose in Context

enonce.texte: Read the following scenarios and write sentences using the infinitive of purpose to describe the actions taken.

questions.texte: Write a sentence for each scenario using the infinitive of purpose.

sousQuestions.texte: Scenario: Marie started a community garden. She wanted to provide fresh vegetables to her neighbors.
sousQuestions.reponse: Marie started a community garden to provide fresh vegetables to her neighbors.

sousQuestions.texte: Scenario: David organized a blood donation drive. He wanted to help people in need.
sousQuestions.reponse: David organized a blood donation drive to help people in need.

sousQuestions.texte: Scenario: Sarah joined a tutoring program. She wanted to help struggling students improve their grades.
sousQuestions.reponse: Sarah joined a tutoring program to help struggling students improve their grades.

sousQuestions.texte: Scenario: The youth club organized a clean-up campaign. They wanted to make the city cleaner.
sousQuestions.reponse: The youth club organized a clean-up campaign to make the city cleaner.

enonce.texte: Listen to a person describing their volunteering experience. Take notes and answer the questions below.

questions.texte: After listening to the audio, answer the following questions.

sousQuestions.texte: What volunteering activity does the speaker participate in?
sousQuestions.reponse: The speaker volunteers at an orphanage (or school/community center).

sousQuestions.texte: Why did the speaker start volunteering? (Use the infinitive of purpose)
sousQuestions.reponse: The speaker started volunteering to help children in need / to make a difference in their lives.

sousQuestions.texte: What benefits has the speaker gained from volunteering?
sousQuestions.reponse: The speaker has gained skills, experience, and personal satisfaction.

sousQuestions.texte: How has volunteering impacted the speaker's life?
sousQuestions.reponse: Volunteering has given the speaker a sense of purpose and has made them more confident and compassionate.

enonce.texte: Write a short paragraph (100-150 words) about a volunteering experience you have had or would like to have. Use the infinitive of purpose at least three times.

questions.texte: Write your paragraph about your volunteering experience.
questions.reponse: A well-structured paragraph using the infinitive of purpose.

sousQuestions.texte: Example: "I started volunteering at a local health center last year. I wanted to help provide medical care to people in need. I also volunteered to gain experience in the healthcare field. Every weekend, I go to the center to assist nurses and to support patients. This experience has been extremely rewarding. I feel a deep sense of satisfaction because I am able to contribute to my community. To continue making a difference, I plan to participate in more health campaigns in the future."
sousQuestions.reponse: Uses the infinitive of purpose (to help, to gain, to assist, to support, to continue) and expresses personal experience.

intitule: Analyzing the Benefits of Community Service

enonce.texte:
Read the following case study about a community service project and answer the questions below.

Case Study: The Douala Community Clean-Up Project

In 2023, a group of young people in Douala launched a community clean-up project. The goal was to improve the sanitation of their neighborhood. Over 100 volunteers participated, collecting more than 5 tons of waste. The project also included recycling workshops to educate residents about waste management.

The clean-up project had several positive outcomes. It improved the appearance of the neighborhood and reduced health risks associated with poor sanitation. Additionally, it created a sense of community pride. Many participants reported gaining new skills and building friendships through the project.

questions.texte: Analyze the case study and answer the following questions.

sousQuestions.texte: What was the main goal of the community clean-up project?
sousQuestions.reponse: To improve the sanitation of the neighborhood.

sousQuestions.texte: What activities were included in the project?
sousQuestions.reponse: Collecting waste and organizing recycling workshops.

sousQuestions.texte: Identify three positive outcomes of this project.
sousQuestions.reponse:
1. Improved appearance of the neighborhood
2. Reduced health risks
3. Created community pride and new skills

sousQuestions.texte: Evaluate the success of this project and suggest one improvement.
sousQuestions.reponse: The project was successful because it achieved its goals of improving sanitation and raising awareness. An improvement could be to organize the clean-up regularly to maintain the results.

enonce.texte: Create a proposal for a community service project in your area. Use the structure below to guide your writing.

questions.texte: Write a community service project proposal.

sousQuestions.texte: Title: Give your project a clear name
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: 'Youth Environmental Action Project'

sousQuestions.texte: Introduction: Describe the problem your project will address
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: 'Our community faces serious waste management problems that affect public health and the environment.'

sousQuestions.texte: Objectives: Explain what you plan to achieve
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: 'We aim to clean up the neighborhood, raise awareness about recycling, and reduce pollution.'

sousQuestions.texte: Activities: Describe the specific activities you will organize
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: 'We will organize weekly clean-up campaigns and recycling workshops.'

sousQuestions.texte: Benefits: Explain who will benefit and how
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: 'Residents will enjoy a cleaner environment, and volunteers will develop important skills.'

sousQuestions.texte: Use the infinitive of purpose at least three times in your proposal
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: 'We will organize clean-ups to improve our neighborhood. We will also provide workshops to educate residents about recycling. We hope to create a cleaner environment to protect public health.'
